meta.topic: Solving Cubic Equations by Factoring and Quadratic Formula
meta.title: Solve x³ − 7x² + 11x = 0 | MAT 170 Exam 2

steps.narration: Solve x cubed minus 7 x squared plus 11 x equals 0.
steps.label: MAT 170 Exam 2 Review

steps.content: Always look for a Greatest Common Factor (GCF) first.
steps.narration: Before doing anything else, always check if there is a greatest common factor you can pull out.
steps.label: Strategy

steps.narration: We start with x cubed minus 7 x squared plus 11 x equals 0.
Every term has an x, so we factor out x as the greatest common factor.

steps.content: Zero Product Property: If A · B = 0, then A = 0 or B = 0.
steps.narration: Now we use the Zero Product Property. If a product equals zero, then at least one of the factors must equal zero.
steps.label: Zero Product Property

steps.narration: We set each factor equal to zero. Either x equals 0, or x squared minus 7 x plus 11 equals 0.
The first factor gives us x equals 0. That is one solution.
For the second factor, x squared minus 7 x plus 11 equals 0, we need the Quadratic Formula because it does not factor nicely.
steps.label: Quadratic Formula

steps.narration: We identify a equals 1, b equals negative 7, and c equals 11.
Substituting into the Quadratic Formula, x equals negative negative 7, plus or minus the square root of negative 7 squared minus 4 times 1 times 11, all over 2 times 1.
Negative negative 7 is 7. Negative 7 squared is 49. And 4 times 1 times 11 is 44. The denominator is 2.
49 minus 44 equals 5. So x equals 7 plus or minus the square root of 5, all over 2.
Our three solutions are x equals 0, x equals 7 plus square root of 5 over 2 which is approximately 4.618, and x equals 7 minus square root of 5 over 2 which is approximately 2.382.
steps.label: Final Answer

steps.narration: Here is the graph of x cubed minus 7 x squared plus 11 x. Notice it crosses the x-axis at all three solutions: x equals 0, approximately 2.382, and approximately 4.618.
